heading: Family-rooted operating experience meets public-markets discipline.
intro: InnerScope's leadership traces directly to the family hearing-care practice that started in the 1950s. The clinic-floor instinct still shapes how the company chooses what to build, what to ship, and which partnerships to enter.

hero_photo.alt: Matthew Moore, Mark Moore, and Kim Moore — InnerScope Hearing Technologies leadership — at the time of the company's public-market debut.
hero_photo.caption: Matthew, Mark, and Kim Moore at the time InnerScope Hearing Technologies first entered the public markets.

leaders.name: Matthew Moore
leaders.title: President & Chief Executive Officer
leaders.bio: Matt has led InnerScope as President and CEO for more than a decade. Under his leadership the company completed the iHEAR Medical and Hearing Assist II LLC acquisitions in 2021, scaled net revenue from roughly $166K in 2020 to $15.28M in 2022 (with $5.58M net profit at a ~36% EBT margin), oversaw the iHEAR Matrix rollout into 4,218 Walmart stores in 2023, and structured InnerScope's 2025 entry into the Ainnova / OTCHealth strategic alliance. His operating perspective is grounded in the family's long history in hearing care, combined with a capital-markets and deal-making mindset.
